title: The Cut
pov_character: Kirin Saleh (primary); Dara (cold open); Curator operative (interlude)
summary: Kirin and Dez reach the maritime transit facility at the edge of the Outside network. The facility's intake officer, Crucita, processes them through a witness-vouching system — not credentials, but names — and Kirin's key authenticates fully for the first time against Rootstock-compatible hardware. In the facility's records, Kirin learns that the corridor's 'missing carrier' Berin Kosta crossed in fourteen months ago and is alive inside. Dez departs, going west to work the infrastructure problem from the corridor side; she leaves Kirin a set of clay route-marker discs. In the transit waiting room, an Outside-born traveler named Marek mentions that Nnedi Eze had a Rootstock key — complicating Dara's accounting. The Curator's operative arrives three hours too late and reports to his controller that the key is now inside the Outside. The Turn: Perun's full Tamsat translation reveals the appointment was made to the key, not the person — whoever holds the key holds the role, with no revocation mechanism. Adaeze's last note, left at the facility for the next bearer in September 2125, waits in Kirin's pocket unopened. Kirin crosses through the transit gate and walks east.
locations: Coastal approach road south of Meridian
Outside — coast track north of gate
themes: bureaucracy as its own kind of seriousness
inheritance as appointment rather than possession
continuity_notes: Tamsat translation complete — 12 of 12 letters rendered. Appointment is key-bound, not person-bound; no revocation mechanism exists in the text. Dez has departed west — infrastructure work, 3 corridor contacts, family-contact situation unchanged. Berin Kosta: alive inside the Outside, admitted Feb 2125; Ade at second way-station can relay contact. Marek (Outside-born, logistics worker, going to Free Zones for first time) mentions Nnedi Eze had a Rootstock key — different form factor, same protocol family. Dara's accounting of 6 original keys may be incomplete (Nnedi's may be a seventh, or may be one of the two Outside custody keys, or the unaccounted-for one; defer to Ep 9-10). Outside census: 7.1M per current internal census vs. 6.2M in the atlas Dara now holds — Adaeze's September 2125 visit predated the June 2125 census. Veritas Structured has gone quiet after the Rootstock market queries — unknown whether they found another avenue or lost confidence. Kirin carries Adaeze's sealed envelope, left Sept 2125 at the facility for the next bearer; unopened. Key behavior: now settled, warm, continuous — a new register distinct from all prior states. Route markers (clay discs, 12 total, Dez's notation) cover first 70 miles from transit gate. Next: five days on foot to first settlement; second way-station, ask for Ade; then plateau settlement, find Nnedi Eze (Adaeze's third instruction).
